notes: Doesn't install. The percentage on the steam client goes to 100% but then when it finishes it doesn't actually show as installed (the game is still greyed out)
title: Assassin’s Creed® Brotherhood

notes:
You need to have the game activated through windows or wine. To bypass the key not loading you need to edit ~/.steam/steam/appcache/appinfo.vdf . Edit the line with the text "legacykeyregistrylocation^@HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Valve\TestApp48195\SteamKey" edit it to "legacykeyregistrylocation2^@HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Valve\TestApp48195\SteamKey". With this typo steam will start the game without loading the game key.

The uplay version bundled with brotherhood is to old to run so you need to manually install the latest version with "WINEPREFIX=/home/{USERNAME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/48190/pfx wine Downloads/UplayInstaller.exe"

After this the game will run fine.
title: Assassin’s Creed® Brotherhood

notes: Runs perfect 60fps with d9vk. I used https://github.com/GloriousEggroll/proton-ge-custom . Bypassed the key not loading with this command 'sed -i 's/legacykeyregistrylocation/legacykeyregistrylocation2/g' ~/.steam/steam/appcache/appinfo.vdf'. That command must be runned everytime before starting steam.
title: Assassin’s Creed® Brotherhood

notes:
Using tweaks described in other comment helped:
[Step one] Changing the line with the text "legacykeyregistrylocation^@HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Valve\TestApp48195\SteamKey" to "legacykeyregistrylocation2^@HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Valve\TestApp48195\SteamKey" in ~/.steam/steam/appcache/appinfo.vdf
[Step two]  Installing the latest version with "WINEPREFIX=/home/{USERNAME}/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/48190/pfx wine the_place_where_you_keep_your_downloaded_uplay_installer/UplayInstaller.exe" 
(WARNING - this will work only after you apply step one and run a game without success. Steam needs to create a wine prefix for this game before you can install Uplay on it)

I downloaded Uplay from official Ubi site (https://ubistatic3-a.akamaihd.net/orbit/launcher_installer/UplayInstaller.exe)
title: Assassin’s Creed® Brotherhood

notes: Same as the other two Gold reports here, except I seem to need to edit the file everytime I (re)launch Steam. uPlay updated itself to the latest version on the second launch, so there's no need to manually update it to the latest version. Also, to get the key to activate the game on uPlay you need to either to run Steam in Windows or in Wine (no need to download the game there, just click on the "CD Key" button in the library)
title: Assassin’s Creed® Brotherhood
